scope.question: What is the complete classification of 4D N=1 asymptotically free quiver gauge theories (1-node and 2-node) that admit a large N limit, with their IR superconformal data (R-charges, central charges, a/c ratios)?
in_scope: Single-node classification (19 theories) as comparison section
Two-node classification (135 universality classes, 4560 theories) from Modules 1-3
R-charges and central charges (a/N^2, c/N^2) for all classes
Conformal window analysis for N_rank2=0 nodes using SQCD criterion
Distribution analysis of central charges and a/c ratios
Comparison with arXiv:2510.19136 for single-node results
out_of_scope: Module 4 boundary analysis (B_a < 0 IR fixed point filter)
Exact symbolic results for the 59 numerical-only classes
Conformal window analysis for nodes with rank-2 matter
unresolved_questions: How to present the 135 classes compactly in JHEP format (tables vs appendix)
Whether distribution of a/c ratios reveals interesting structure worth highlighting

user_asserted_anchors: Single-node results must be consistent with arXiv:2510.19136
known_good_baselines: SQCD conformal window 3/2 < N_f/N_c < 3 for single-node SU(N)
context_gaps: No independent benchmark exists for two-node results
crucial_inputs: Distribution plots of a/N^2 and a/c ratios
Conformal window classification for N_rank2=0 nodes

formulations: Large N leading order with equal-rank assumption for all nodes

observables.name: Complete classification table
observables.definition: All 1-node and 2-node N=1 AF quiver gauge theories with large N limit, listing gauge groups, matter content, R-charges, a/N^2, c/N^2, a/c

observables.name: Central charge distribution
observables.definition: Distribution (histogram) of a/N^2 and a/c ratios across all classified theories

observables.name: Conformal window classification
observables.definition: For each N_rank2=0 node, whether x = N_bif/2 falls inside SQCD conformal window 3/2 < x < 3

claims.statement: We present the complete classification of 1-node and 2-node 4D N=1 AF quiver gauge theories admitting a large N limit, with their superconformal R-charges and central charges from a-maximization
The distribution of central charges and a/c ratios across classified theories reveals structural patterns
Conformal window analysis identifies which N_rank2=0 nodes lie inside/outside the SQCD conformal window at large N

deliverables.description: JHEP paper presenting the complete classification
Tables of all universality classes with gauge pair, matter content, R-charges, a/N^2, c/N^2, a/c
Histograms of a/N^2 and a/c ratio distributions
Table of N_rank2=0 morphologies with conformal window status

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-complete-classification
acceptance_tests.procedure: Compare single-node classification results against arXiv:2510.19136
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All overlapping theories agree on R-charges and central charges

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-complete-classification
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify a-theorem (a_UV >= a_IR), check a/c > 0, verify anomaly-free conditions hold
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No violations found across all classified theories

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-conformal-window
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify SQCD conformal window reproduces known 3/2 < N_f/N_c < 3 for single SU(N) node

references.why_it_matters: Contains partial single-node classification to compare against

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-complete-classification
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Raw classification tables without distribution analysis or conformal window filtering
forbidden_proxies.reason: Tables alone are a catalog, not physics — the distribution and conformal window analysis give the paper scientific content

weakest_anchors: Comparison with 2510.19136 is partial — they do not have the full single-node classification
unvalidated_assumptions: Equal-rank assumption (all nodes share same N)
Large N leading order is sufficient for classification
disconfirming_observations: If single-node results disagree with 2510.19136 on overlapping theories, the a-maximization pipeline has a bug

open_questions: How to present the 135 classes compactly in JHEP format (tables vs appendix)
Whether distribution of a/c ratios reveals interesting structure worth highlighting
